MESSAGE: 429 error indicates that the API rate limit has been reached
SYMPTOM: Results/ data will come back incomplete, and requests may be blocked temporarily.
INFO: To retrieve the data more efficiently and avoid hitting the limits. We recommend increasing the limit parameter in your API calls (for example using a limit of around 5000 records per request) and using pagination accordingly. This reduces the total number of API calls required while still allowing you to pull all necessary data within a reasonable time frame. At this time the rate limits cannot be relaxed, but adjusting the request size should help avoid further 429 responses

SYMPTOM: This error generally indicates that the installation failed due to file access restrictions or temporary system conditions while executing the update file. ConnectSecure patching utilizes WSUS to execute patches with the required privileges. We recommend verifying that there are no file locks or pending system reboots that might interfere with the update installation.

MESSAGE: ôGet-LocalUser | ForEach-Object { $userName = $_.Name $output = net user $userName $lastLogonLine = $output | Where-Object { $_ -match '^ *Last logon' } if ($lastLogonLine) { if ($lastLogonLine -match 'Last logon\s+(.*)') { $lastLogon = $matches[1].Trim() } else { $lastLogon = \"Not found\" } } else { $lastLogon = \"Not found\" } [PSCustomObject]@{ UserName = $userName LastLogon = $lastLogon } } | ConvertTo-Json -Compress " ParentUser: TCT\tctrtm.ö
SYMPTOM: WithSecure or Adlumin detects the NET USER commands but does not identify CS as the cause.
INFO: EDR platforms including Adlumin and WithSecure attribute activity based on the immediate process such as powershell.exe or net.exe and do not always retain full parent process lineage. As a result EDR telemetry may not explicitly identify ConnectSecureAgent as the initiator even though the activity originates from a ConnectSecure scan.

MESSAGE: Connector Failed - status_code : 460
SYMPTOM: "When integrating API with a SIEM, they may get this error when testing the API ."
INFO: For the authorization, could you please ensure the tenant name is included in lowercase (for example: lowercasename) while generating the token? The format should be: (tenantname+client_id) ? then base64 encode and use it in the Client-Auth-Token field. Once done, please click Execute and verify.

MESSAGE: INT API failed with status code 429
SYMPTOM: Partner will not be able to scan fortigate firewall due to rate limit exceeded.
INFO: FortiGate REST API enforces built-in rate limits and will return an HTTP 429 status code when these are exceeded. By default, FortiGate allows approximately: 100 GET/monitor API calls per second; 30 configuration write calls per second. If these thresholds are crossed, the firewall will block additional requests from the same source IP using a sliding time window. To resolve this: Please ensure that requests from the Agent/Probe system are prioritized and not restricted by the firewall. Consider applying rate limiting or distributing API calls over time to prevent hitting the FortiGate rate limit. Create a new profile with new REST API key and map it in the same company where the issue is occurring, and remove the old credential from the ConnectSecure Portal. After mapping the new credentials, initiate a fresh firewall scan.
REF: Please find the KB link to create new REST API key.
